hx_brief: 62yo male with sudden onset tearing chest pain radiating to the back.

hints.hx: Pain is 'tearing' and 10/10. Patient has long-standing untreated HTN.
hints.pe: Blood pressure discrepancy of >20mmHg between arms. New early diastolic murmur.
hints.img: CTA Chest: Clearly visualized intimal tear with a 'true' and 'false' lumen.

lab_results.Specialty: Significantly elevated (3,500 ng/mL); non-specific for origin.

hx_brief: 24yo female with periumbilical pain that migrated to the RLQ.

hints.hx: Started as dull midline pain; now sharp in the RLQ. Nausea and anorexia present.
hints.pe: Positive Psoas sign and Rovsing sign. Guarding in the right lower quadrant.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Dilated appendix >7mm with wall thickening and fat stranding.

hx_brief: 55yo female with sudden severe 'pounding' headache and blurry vision.

hints.hx: Pain is orbital. Patient mentions seeing 'rainbow halos' around the light bulbs.
hints.pe: Right eye: Steamy/cloudy cornea. Pupil is mid-dilated and non-reactive to light.
hints.img: Slit-lamp: Total occlusion of the iridocorneal angle.

hx_brief: 32yo male with 2 days of progressive weakness in his legs moving upward.

hints.hx: Recently recovered from a 'stomach flu' with bloody diarrhea 2 weeks ago.
hints.pe: Lower extremity strength 1/5. Absent patellar and Achilles reflexes.
hints.img: MRI Spine: Enhancement of the cauda equina nerve roots.

lab_results.Specialty: Albuminocytologic dissociation (High Protein, Normal Cell Count).

hx_brief: 68yo male with painless gross hematuria for three weeks.

hints.hx: Ex-smoker. Patient mentions working in a dye manufacturing plant for 25 years.
hints.pe: No palpable masses. Non-tender abdomen. Normal DRE.
hints.img: Cystoscopy: Multiple exophytic, cauliflower-like lesions on the trigone.

lab_results.UA: Numerous RBCs; No casts; No dysmorphic RBCs

hx_brief: 45yo obese female with RUQ pain radiating to the right shoulder.

hints.hx: Pain occurs roughly 30 minutes after eating fatty foods. Nausea is severe.
hints.pe: Positive Murphy’s sign. Patient stops breathing mid-inspiration during RUQ palpation.
hints.img: HIDA Scan: Non-visualization of the gallbladder after tracer injection.

lab_results.Specialty: Mildly elevated AST/ALT; Normal Total Bilirubin.

hx_brief: 19yo male athlete collapses and dies during a basketball game.

hints.hx: Patient was previously healthy but his father died of 'heart issues' at age 30.
hints.pe: 4/6 Crescendo-decrescendo murmur at LLSB. Murmur softens when squatting.
hints.img: Echo: Marked ventricular septal thickening; Systolic Anterior Motion (SAM) of mitral valve.

lab_results.EKG: Deep narrow 'dagger' Q waves in lateral leads
lab_results.Specialty: Identified mutation in Sarcomere protein (Beta-myosin heavy chain).

hx_brief: 28yo female with a 3-month history of progressive exertional dyspnea and Raynaud phenomenon.

hints.hx: No history of smoking or asthma. Recent onset of tight, shiny skin over her fingers (sclerodactyly).
hints.pe: Lungs are clear to auscultation. Precordial heave and a loud S2 (pulmonic component) at the left upper sternal border.
hints.img: Right Heart Cath: Mean Pulmonary Artery Pressure (mPAP) is 30 mmHg at rest (Normal <20).

hx_brief: 42yo male with 2 weeks of worsening thirst, frequent urination, and deep, aching bone pain.

hints.hx: Patient has a history of 'neck surgery' 5 years ago for a thyroid nodule. Constipation and abdominal moans.
hints.pe: Decreased deep tendon reflexes. Mild muscle weakness. No palpable neck masses.
hints.img: X-ray: Subperiosteal bone resorption in the phalanges; 'Salt and pepper' skull.

hx_brief: 52yo male with 'recurrent pneumonia' and persistent hemoptysis for 4 months.

hints.hx: 30-pack-year smoker. Reports a new 'whistling' sound when breathing. Recently noted his shirts feel tight around the neck.
hints.pe: Dullness to percussion in right upper lobe. Right arm is noticeably more edematous than the left.
hints.img: CT Chest: Central hilar mass causing narrowing of the right mainstem bronchus.

lab_results.Specialty: Histology: Intercellular bridges and Keratin pearls.

hx_brief: 31yo female with acute onset of severe abdominal pain and dark urine after a night of drinking.

hints.hx: Patient has had similar episodes of abdominal pain but no cause was found. Denies any rashes or joint pain.
hints.pe: Abdomen is soft, non-tender, and non-distended despite '10/10' pain. No rebound or guarding.
hints.img: CT Abdomen: Completely unremarkable (Negative for appendicitis/gallstones).

lab_results.UA: Negative for blood; Turns purple/dark after standing in light.

hx_brief: 65yo male presents with 3 days of localized back pain and sudden onset urinary incontinence.

hints.hx: History of prostate cancer (Gleason 8). He describes 'electric-like' pains shooting down both legs.
hints.pe: Tenderness over T10-T12 vertebrae. Decreased anal sphincter tone. Hyperreflexia in lower extremities.
hints.img: MRI Spine: Epidural mass compressing the spinal cord at the thoracic level.

hx_brief: 14yo boy with sudden onset of high fever, petechial rash on ankles, and severe headache.

hints.hx: Just returned from a summer camping trip in North Carolina. Denies neck stiffness.
hints.pe: Maculopapular rash on wrists and ankles, some lesions are starting to become petechial.
hints.img: CXR: Normal. CT Head: Normal (Negative for hemorrhage/mass).

hx_brief: 38yo female with 2 weeks of 'crushing' fatigue, diffuse bone pain, and gum bleeding.

hints.hx: Patient feels 'unusually cold' lately. Denies travel or recent infections.
hints.pe: Pale conjunctiva. Petechiae on the trunk. Gingival hyperplasia (swollen gums).
hints.img: Bone Marrow: Hypercellular marrow with >20% myeloblasts.

lab_results.Specialty: Myeloperoxidase (MPO) positive; Auer rods visualized in cytoplasm.
